entry.title: CQN subquery not working for local/external services in CAP
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm using CAP(Node.js) with odata(v4) & fiori elements. I'm trying to use CQN subqueries and execute them at service level i.e. using srv.run(query) , srv here is a service object created by cds.connect.to('ServiceName'). According to Capire documentation you can use a query as source in the from property : https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/cds/cqn#from

This works but only when you run the query using cds.run(query). My requirement is to execute the query in other services i.e. srv.run(). But when i try doing that it gives the following error : [cds] - TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map')

And when you click on the file( resolveView.js i.e. an internal file) that the error is referring to, it shows the following code , error is on line 653: 

 Seems like it expects an object with ref property as key and an array as value i.e from = {ref : ['TableName']}, and works only if the source is a table & not another query. Is this the default behavior and is there any way around it ?

entry.title: Warum Change Management bei einer SAP Cloud Migration wichtig ist - ein Blick in die Praxis
entry.content:
In der aktuellen Folge des EducationNewsCast Podcast diskutierte ich mit Philipp Blaesing von Siemens Healthineers und Anja Scheurer von SAP die entscheidende Rolle des Change Managements bei SAP Cloud Migrationen, insbesondere im Rahmen des RISE-Programms. Hier könnt ihr reinhören auf 🎧 SAP Podcasts - Apple - Spotify. Anbei eine Zusammenfassung als Blog. 

Das RISE-Projekt bei Siemens Healthineers: Eine globale IT-Initiative

Das RISE-Projekt bei Siemens Healthineers ist eine der größten IT-Initiativen des Unternehmens. Es umfasst die Migration von über 20 SAP-Systemen und angrenzenden Applikationen in die SAP Private Cloud.

Philipp Blaesing betont: "Innovation hat bei uns einen hohen Stellenwert bei Siemens Healthineers. Und da in Zukunft alle Innovationen in der RISE Cloud entwickelt werden, hat natürlich auch die Migration bei uns eine sehr hohe Visibility und Wichtigkeit."

Technische Migration vs. Geschäftsprozess-Transformation: Ein zweistufiger Ansatz

Ein interessanter Aspekt des Projekts ist die Trennung von technischer Migration und Geschäftsprozess-Transformation. Blaesing erklärt: "Wir trennen die technische Migration von den Business-Transformationsprojekten. [...] Erst ein stabiles Fundament gießen, wenn man mal so eine Metapher von einem Hausbau sieht und dann kann man die Räume draufbauen, wie man sie will."

Obwohl es sich primär um ein technisches Projekt handelt, unterstreicht Anja Scheurer die Bedeutung des Change Managements:

"Auch wenn RISE-Projekte auf den ersten Blick eben nur als ein technisches Projekt scheinen, betreffen technologische Veränderungen, technologische Transformationen immer die Menschen, die sie dann schlussendlich auch natürlich anwenden müssen."

Die Rolle des Change Managements in technischen Migrationsprojekten

Die Zusammenarbeit zwischen Projekt- und Change Management ist eng verzahnt. Anja Scheurer erläutert: "Grundsätzlich arbeiten wir eng auf Programmebene, sowohl aber auch eben auf der Projektebene mit den jeweiligen Verantwortlichen zusammen." Das Change Management-Team fungiert als Cross-Workstream im Programm und gibt die Methodik und den Change- und User-Kommunikationsplan auf Programmebene vor.

Die Verbindung von Change- und Projekt Management 

Change Management und Projektmanagement waren bei der SAP Cloud Migration von Siemens Healthineers eng verzahnt:

  • Integration: Change Management als Cross-Workstream im Gesamtprogramm.
  • Klare Rollen: Deutliche Aufgabenteilung zwischen PMO und Change Team.
  • Operative Einbindung: Change-Experten in Projektterminen und Cutover-Meetings.
  • Kommunikation: Während das PMO die interne Programmkommunikation vorantrieb, fokussierte sich das Change Management Team auf die Nutzer- und Nutzenkommunikation.
  • Ressourcenbereitstellung: Das Change Management Team stellte den Projektleitern Werkzeuge und Materialien zur Verfügung, um Change-Aktivitäten umzusetzen.

Diese enge Zusammenarbeit ermöglichte eine durchgängige Berücksichtigung von Change-Aspekten und schnelle Reaktionen auf Herausforderungen im Projektverlauf. 

Bewährte Methoden und Tools im Change Management

Bei den eingesetzten Methoden und Tools hat sich das Team anfangs am ADKAR-Modell orientiert, dieses aber an die spezifischen Bedürfnisse des Projekts angepasst. Bewährt haben sich klassische Stakeholder-Analysen, Engagement-Pläne und Change-Readiness-Checks. Blaesing betont auch die Wichtigkeit eines guten People-Networks und vielfältiger Kommunikationskanäle.

Learnings und Tipps für erfolgreiche SAP Migrationsprojekte

Als wichtigste Lernerfahrung nennt Philipp Blaesing die frühzeitige Einbindung des Change Managements:

".. auf jeden Fall das Change Management so früh wie möglich zu involvieren, also schon in den Planungsphasen." Anja Scheurer ergänzt: "Unterschätzt den Bedarf einfach überhaupt nicht für das Change Management und der Kommunikation in Migrationsprojekten, bindet eben die Kollegen und Kolleginnen früh ein."

Auch gaben beide konkrete Tipps für die Praxis:

  • Eine klare RACI-Matrix für Change- und Kommunikations-verantwortlichkeiten erstellen
  • Vielfältige Kommunikationskanäle nutzen (Newsletter, Webseiten, Q&A-Sessions ... )
  • Regelmäßige Feedback-Runden mit Stakeholdern durchführen
  • Change Management als offiziellen Cross-Workstream im Programm etablieren

Inspirierende Aussagen aus dem Podcast

"Wenn man das Gefühl hat, man kommuniziert zu viel, dann war es richtig."
"Change Management ist sehr viel mehr in den Mittelpunkt gerückt als vor fünf bis zehn Jahren und gewinnt immer mehr an Wichtigkeit."

Resümee

Die Erfahrungen von Siemens Healthineers zeigen, dass eine frühzeitige Einbindung, klare Strukturen und vielfältige Kommunikation entscheidend sind. 

Was ich wichtig finde ist auch folgendes: Change Management erweist sich auch bei primär technischen Projekten als wichtiger Erfolgsfaktor. Die Handlungsfelder sind vielleicht etwas anders. So gibt es beispielsweise wenig End-User Training. Aber Änderungen die Prozesse, Jobs und Aufgaben betreffen gibt es auch bei technischen Projekten. Und auch Fragen zum Mehrwert eines solchen technischen Projekts und die Besprechung und Kommunikation dieser Vorteile. Und hier ist eben Change Management sehr wichtig mit all seinen Facetten, von Stakeholder Analysen bis Kommunikation

⁉️ Habt ihr weitere Beispiele, was bei sehr technischen Projekten besonders ist rund um Change Management? Freue mich auf Kommentare hier auch. 

Shownotes

Buch: Die 5 Sprachen der Mitarbeitermotivation

Buch: Der Change Code von Dieter Lederer

Doku der Community-Session mit Anja zum SAP Change Starterkit

SAP News: Siemens Healthineers entscheidet sich für RISE with SAP um Leistung zu steigern

Aus-und Weiterbildung bei Siemens Healthineers

entry.title: Re: create a new additional product description for diffe...
entry.content:
The Migration Object 'Product - extend existing record by new org levels' allows to create new descriptions for a Product

 

Please check the resource Product - extend existing record by new org levels  : 'You can also maintain new descriptions, new units of measure, and new GTINs.'

 

The Mass update functionality included in 'Mass Maintenance for Master Data' allows also creation as well as change using the _ACTION_CODE" = “I” outlined at Specific Syntax for Dynamic File Upload

entry.title: SSO Error in SAC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need your help.

I have configured IAS as a proxy, and in IAS, I have set the Subject Name Identifier=employee_id

In Azure (OpenID Connection to IAS), I have added the employee_id to Additional Claims.

In the source system (Azure) in IPS, I have configured the transformation as follows:

{
"sourcePath": "$.employeeId",
"targetPath": "$.userName",
"correlationAttribute": true
}

Also in IPS transformation I replaced "sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName" with "sourcePath": "$.employeeId".

Users are provisioned with IPS.

In IAS, the "Login Name" field for users receives the employee_id, for example, 1111. These users are then provisioned to SAC and have the employee_id (1111) in the "USER ID" field as well.

When I try to test SSO and select "Choose a user attribute to map to your Identity Provider" -> User attribute set = "USER ID", the user is not found in SAC.

The user accesses the SAC link and enters their email address. The user is authenticated in Azure, but in SAC, the user is not found.

I need help.

I can't solve this by myself.

Thank you very much !!!

entry.title: Why the CFO and the Office of the CFO Should Care About Procurement
entry.content:
In today's dynamic business environment, procurement has evolved from a mere administrative function to a strategic asset that significantly impacts an organization's financial health and sustainability. For Chief Financial Officers (CFOs) and their offices, prioritizing procurement can lead to enhanced financial performance, risk mitigation, and value creation.

 

Enhancing Financial Performance through Procurement

Cost Management and Efficiency

Procurement plays a crucial role in managing both direct and indirect costs. For instance, a multinational conglomerate in the manufacturing industry saves $1 billion annually by localizing its supply chain and negotiating better terms with suppliers. Similarly, a tech giant's procurement team cuts indirect costs by 15% through consolidated purchasing agreements. By effectively managing these costs, CFOs can allocate resources more efficiently and improve the organization's bottom line.

Improving Cash Flow

Effective procurement strategies can improve cash flow by extending payment terms and optimizing inventory levels. For example, a consumer electronics company’s just-in-time procurement model reduces the need for large inventory holdings, freeing up capital for other strategic investments. Improved cash flow management enhances the organization's liquidity position and financial stability, allowing CFOs to invest in growth opportunities.

Enhancing Operational Efficiency

Streamlined procurement processes contribute to overall operational efficiency. Automated procurement systems reduce manual intervention, minimize errors, and speed up the procurement cycle. This efficiency translates into shorter lead times, ensuring that the organization can respond swiftly to market demands and opportunities. Enhancing operational efficiency allows CFOs to drive productivity and reduce operational costs, leading to improved financial performance.

Risk Mitigation

Supply Chain Resilience

The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the vulnerabilities in global supply chains. A diversified supplier base and robust contingency plans help companies maintain operations during disruptions. A resilient supply chain ensures uninterrupted operations, which is critical for maintaining revenue streams and customer satisfaction. By fostering supply chain resilience, CFOs can mitigate operational risks and safeguard the organization's financial stability.

Compliance and Regulatory Adherence

Procurement must adhere to various regulatory requirements, including anti-bribery, anti-corruption, and environmental regulations. Ensuring compliance with these regulations helps avoid legal penalties and reputational damage. Moreover, ethical procurement practices foster trust with stakeholders, enhancing the organization's overall reputation. Ensuring compliance and ethical practices enables CFOs to protect the organization from legal and reputational risks.

Managing Market Volatility

Procurement professionals monitor commodity prices and market trends to anticipate and mitigate the impact of price volatility on the organization's cost structure. By securing long-term contracts and hedging against price fluctuations, procurement helps stabilize costs, allowing CFOs to forecast financial performance more accurately. Managing market volatility supports CFOs in maintaining financial predictability and stability.

Value Creation

Innovation and Competitive Advantage

Strategic procurement drives innovation and creates competitive advantages. Collaborating with suppliers on new product development and process improvements introduces cutting-edge technologies and practices that differentiate the organization in the market. This innovation can lead to new revenue streams and enhanced market positioning. Leveraging procurement for innovation allows CFOs to drive business growth and maintain a competitive edge.

Supplier Relationship Management

Building strong relationships with key suppliers is crucial for long-term success. Effective supplier relationship management (SRM) leads to preferential treatment, early access to new products, and favorable pricing. Prioritizing SRM as a strategic initiative maximizes the value derived from supplier partnerships. Strong supplier relationships enable CFOs to secure better terms and drive cost efficiencies.

Sustainability and Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR)

Sustainable procurement practices align with the organization's CSR goals. By sourcing from environmentally responsible suppliers and promoting fair labor practices, procurement contributes to the organization's sustainability agenda. Emphasizing sustainability in procurement helps CFOs meet CSR objectives and strengthen the organization's reputation.

Data-Driven Decision Making

Leveraging Procurement Analytics

Procurement generates a wealth of data that can inform strategic decision-making. Advanced analytics tools provide insights into spending patterns, supplier performance, and market trends. These insights drive more informed decisions, optimize procurement strategies, and identify opportunities for cost savings and efficiency improvements. Leveraging data analytics allows CFOs to make evidence-based decisions that enhance financial performance.

Integration with Financial Planning

Integrating procurement data with financial planning and analysis (FP&A) processes ensures that procurement activities align with the organization's financial goals. This alignment enables CFOs to create more accurate budgets, forecasts, and financial models, ultimately driving better financial performance. Integrated financial planning supports CFOs in achieving strategic objectives and maximizing value.

Conclusion

Procurement is no longer just an administrative function but a strategic asset for CFOs. By prioritizing procurement, CFOs can achieve cost savings, mitigate risks, and drive innovation. Strategic procurement practices enhance financial performance, ensure compliance, and foster sustainable growth, making it an indispensable part of the CFO's agenda.

entry.title: Single order process order process
entry.content:
Single order process order process

Single Order:

It is a warehouse process designed to handle individual sales orders efficiently. It ensures that each order is processed separately, optimizing picking, packing, and shipping for high-priority or time-sensitive deliveries. This approach minimizes errors, improves order accuracy, and enhances customer satisfaction by streamlining warehouse operations.

Process Order:
A process order in SAP EWM is used to oversee the execution of production and manufacturing processes within the warehouse. It seamlessly integrates with SAP Production Planning (PP) to coordinate material movements required for production. SAP EWM enhances inventory management and material flow, ensuring efficient handling and optimized warehouse operations based on process order requirements.

Warehouse Task:
A warehouse task in SAP EWM is an operational instruction assigned to workers for executing specific activities such as picking, putaway, or stock transfers. It is automatically generated based on inbound, outbound, or internal warehouse processes, ensuring seamless execution. By systematically managing these tasks, SAP EWM enhances efficiency, accuracy, and productivity in warehouse operations.

Steps for creation of the Single order process order:

Step 1:

Create New Process order using the transaction code CO01

Process order is created.

 

Step 2:

Check the PMR in Transaction /N/SCWM/PMR

Provide the process order and then click on the execute button.

 

Step 3:

Perform Staging in Transaction /N/SCWM/STAGE

Step 4:

Check the Warehouse order linked to Process order

Warehouse order is linked to Process order

Confirm the warehouse order using the confirmation transaction code /n/scwm/to_conf

 

Conclusion:

Single order process order process is provided.

entry.title: Re: Manually Editing the Amount in Settlement Document| S...
entry.content:
Hello Priya,

You can influence the amount in the settlement document by adding condition types in the condition contract before automatic settlement. Below is a text from the SAP help portal:

The customer may raise concerns about the sales rebate amount that you calculated in a partial settlement or in the final settlement. In such cases, it is advisable to adjust your settlement after you have come to an agreement with your customer regarding the settlement data. You can use the following condition types to adjust the sales rebate amount:

- REBD for Rebate BusVol Delta

- REJ1 for Rebate Adjustment

- REV1 for Rebate Verified

With condition type REBD, you can correct the business volume. The rebate condition RES1 is then applied to the sum of the business volume REBV determined by the system and the delta business volume REBD. Condition type REJ1 has the calculation type of Fixed Amount. It represents an additional sales rebate amount on top of the amount calculated with RES1. You can also combine corrections with REBD and REJ1. Condition type REV1 represents the finally agreed upon sales rebate amount. The calculation type for REV1 is also Fixed Amount. Based on the exclusion rules for the pricing procedure, the own calculation of the sales rebate amount is deactivated and REV1 becomes the actual net sales rebate amount.

You maintain the corrective condition types in the condition contract with a suitable validity. When you want to adjust a partial settlement, you can specify the date of the next settlement for the adjustment. To adjust a final settlement, you can perform a delta settlement by selecting the final settlement date as the condition validity. If you need to consult with your customer beforehand, you can use the corrective condition types already present in the final settlement. In such cases, you can also specify an execution date for the final settlement after the validity end of the condition contract.

With regards to the simulation, reports WB2R_SC and WB2R_SV have simulate option under Run Type, just make sure you select List Range either list output header data or list output header and item data. On the results screen you can see the calculated amounts as sub-total fields and you can see the expected rebate.

Thanks

entry.title: Custom Time Dependent Table Date Validations
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

However, for a project, My client has requested to insert a department/group as responsible partner and not just a sap user, and to all the people inside that department/group, send the notification or task. I have try with the manage teams and responsabilities app, but we did not find a solution. The last an unique way that I know it could work is doing it from the customizing, as in the private and in the on-premise solution it is possible to do it, and this request would be solved doing this process.

I need your help in order to understand if there is another workaround for this problem in which I can insert a department of the company inside a notification as a partner.

Maybe I found a way to solve this, using the position responsible ST type of partner. The problem is that there is no data available in the search help. There is no data for position responsible and I can not find a way to insert data for this type of partner. Do you know how to do it? Could you help me with this?

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Implementation: Module Sequence and Logic
entry.content:
It is important to note that the sequence of SAP SuccessFactors implementation depends on business needs. What I have is not a prescription but a suggested approach with explanations based on my years of experience.

Implementation Considerations

  • Small organizations may start with Recruiting, Onboarding, and Performance & Goals without EC.
  • Enterprises/Large organizations often prioritize EC first, then gradually roll out talent, reporting and payroll modules.

Follow a structured sequence to ensure smooth integration, data consistency, and minimal disruption to business operations. My best practice follows this order:

Note:  I am fully aware of SAP groupings 1. Core HR & payroll 2. Talent 3. HR analytics and workforce planning

1. Core HR (Foundation Module)

  • Employee Central (EC) – This acts as the backbone of SuccessFactors, managing employee master data, organizational structures, job classifications, and position management. EC is usually implemented first since most other modules depend on it for employee records and data integrity. (especially when replacing an existing HR system). Centralized system for managing employee information, such as personal details, job history, organizational structure, time off, and other HR-related data.

2. Talent Acquisition

  • Recruiting Marketing (RMK) – Focuses on employer branding, career site management, and candidate attraction. RMK is often implemented before or alongside Recruiting Management to ensure a steady flow of applicants. Its a tool for creating and distributing employer branding content to attract top talent, manage job postings, and engage with candidates effectively.

  • Recruiting Management (RCM) – Handles job requisitions, applicant tracking, interview scheduling, and hiring processes. It integrates closely with RMK and Employee Central to streamline recruitment. Manages the core recruiting processes. Increasing recruitment efficiency empowers to attract, engage, and hire talent seamlessly. Key features job posting, candidate sourcing, applicant tracking, and interview scheduling.

  • Onboarding (ONB 2.0) – Manages new hire processes, including paperwork, compliance, and preboarding activities. It connects with EC to automatically create employee profiles after hiring. ONB is often implemented before or alongside RCM. Simplify employee onboarding, cross-boarding, offboarding, and rehire programs with one comprehensive solution.

3. Talent Management

  • Performance & Goals (PMGM) – Facilitates performance reviews, goal setting, and feedback processes. It requires EC for reporting structures and employee records. Assist in aligning strategy and goals, enhancing employee performance via continuous coaching and feedback, and acknowledging top talent

  • Compensation (CMP) – Manages salary, bonuses, and incentive programs. It integrates with EC for pay structures and PMGM for performance-based rewards. Optimize workforce compensation and align compensation programs with business goals, enhancing individual and business performance through personalized rewards

  • Succession & Development (S&D) – Helps identify future leaders and plan career growth. It relies on EC for position structures and PMGM for performance history. Identifying and developing talent within organizations to ensure smooth leadership transitions and foster career growth opportunities

  • Learning Management System (LMS) – Provides employee training, certifications, and development programs. It integrates with EC for user profiles and with S&D for personalized learning paths. Empower individuals for the future by leveraging innovative capabilities that drive skills management, learning, and development.

4. Workforce Analytics & Planning

  • Workforce Analytics (WFA) – Generates HR insights using data from EC, Recruiting, Performance, and Compensation.

  • Workforce Planning (WFP) – Supports long-term workforce strategies and forecasting.

5. Payroll & Time Management

  • Employee Central Payroll (ECP) – Manages payroll processing, tax compliance, and salary disbursements. It requires EC for employee data and Compensation for salary details.

  • Time Tracking (TT) – Tracks working hours, absences, and leave management. It integrates with EC for work schedules and payroll for time-based payments.

Logic Behind the Implementation Sequence

The above sequence follows a logical, functional and technical dependency approach, ensuring that foundational elements are in place before dependent modules are deployed. Below is my reasoning behind this order:

1. Core HR (Employee Central) 1st to Go-Live

  • Single Source of Truth – EC centralizes employee data, preventing duplication across modules.

  • Data Integrity – All other modules (talent, payroll, analytics) depend on EC’s organizational structure, reporting lines, and job classifications.

  • Smooth Integrations – Without EC, other modules would lack a structured HR framework, leading to manual workarounds.

2. Talent Acquisition 2nd (Recruiting Marketing, Recruiting & Onboarding)

  • Recruiting Marketing (RMK) First – This module focuses on employer branding and talent attraction. Implementing RMK before RCM ensures that a talent pipeline is established early.

  • Recruiting Management (RCM) & Onboarding (ONB) Follow – Once candidates are attracted via RMK, RCM manages hiring, and ONB streamlines the transition of new hires into EC.

  • Avoiding Standalone Recruiting Systems – Implementing RCM without EC can lead to fragmented data and duplicate employee records.

  • From an implementation and go-live standpoint, my recommendation is to proceed with the go-live of all three together.

3. Talent Management Modules 3rd

  • Performance & Goals Before Compensation – Performance data is often used to determine salary increments and bonuses, so PMGM is implemented before Compensation.

  • Compensation Before Succession & Development – Compensation relies on EC for pay structures and on PMGM for performance-based pay.

  • Succession & Development After Compensation – Career planning and leadership development are tied to performance and compensation trends.

  • Opportunity Marketplace (OM)- Sequence TBD see note below

  • LMS After EC – Learning paths are linked to job roles, requiring EC for structured employee profiles. LMS sometimes can be a beast, especially for Validated life science clients also migrating existing training data, user profiles, and historical records from legacy systems to LMS can be super complex. LMS is not directly dependent, but a connection can be drawn to Performance & Goals, which is used for performance reviews and goal management and can be linked to learning activities, as well as Succession and Career Development can integrate with LMS to align learning and development with career paths.

  • From an implementation and go-live standpoint, I recommend going live with all three modules—PMGM, CMP, and S&D together, especially if the LMS requirements are complex.

Note I have never seen any of my clients utilize Opportunity Marketplace + I have limited knowledge in this newer module

Opportunity Marketplace (OM) – provides employees with opportunities to apply for internal roles, projects, and other career growth options. Opportunity Marketplace integrates with both EC for employee data and PMGM for performance assessments, as well as LMS for ensuring employees have the required skills for opportunities. My understanding follows Succession & Development because the S&D module identifies potential future leaders and high-potential employees

4. Workforce Analytics & Planning 4th

  • Requires Data from Core HR and Talent Management – Implementing WFA before EC or talent modules would result in incomplete reports.

  • Workforce Planning Needs Succession Data – WFP helps plan for future workforce needs based on succession trends and business growth.

5. Payroll & Time Management Last

  • Time Tracking Requires Organizational Rules – Work schedules, policies, and overtime rules must be set up in EC before Time Tracking.

  • Payroll Depends on EC & Compensation – Payroll calculations require accurate employee records and salary details from EC and Compensation. Payroll is complex and highly regulated. Deploying it last ensures that core HR and compensation structures are stable before processing salaries.

This method ensures that each module is implemented in a way that maximizes efficiency and minimizes risk. Organizations should tailor this sequence based on their specific needs, but following these principles guarantees a more streamlined and successful implementation.

entry.title: HYR2502 upgrade issue- Error in Account Determination of GL Account - Urgent
entry.content:
After the latest system upgrade to release 2502, we are facing an issue with new postings to a GL account. The error "Account is Blocked for Posting" is raised and the GL account involved is a zero balance clearing account. 

The error is mostly encountered as "Error in Account Determination for zero balance clearing account" and "Account is Blocked for Posting" due to which it is not possible to make postings to the GL account and post the journal entries. 

Checking the account masterdata it is noted that the account has Blocked for posting=Yes setting enabled. 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: HYR2502 upgrade issue- Error in Account Determination...
entry.content:
In the new 2502 release, SAP Public Cloud Development have introduced a new check for zero-balance clearing accounts to check whether the derived account is blocked for posting. An error is raised if it is found to be blocked.   

This is in contrast to previous releases where it was possible to make postings to zero-balance clearing accounts even if the account has setting Blocked for posting=Yes in the account masterdata. The previous logic in releases 2408 and before allowed this as zero-balance clearing accounts are not posted to directly in entry view, they are posted to in general ledger view indirectly only due to document splitting requirements. 

As a result in this change in logic, all zero-balance clearing accounts in 2502 production tenants should not have a blocked status in the account masterdata in order to allow ongoing postings. 

Please refer to the following KBA for more details 2607276 - Master data for zero-balance clearing account

entry.title: Re: Invoice before goods issue in SAP S4 HANA without EWM
entry.content:
A pro forma invoice is a preliminary bill or estimate that's sent before a sale is final. It's a non-binding document that's used to request payment for goods or services that will be delivered in the future. 

What I have seen is a new Form created on the Delivery Output message --that will print the performs invoice -- so it is like setting up a new output type for delivery output -- you can use a different method to control the output e.g. requirement profile  such as once picking is completed or no need to use it -depends upon your requirements -- also you can setup condition records with access sequence assigned to a control which parameters will trigger the output record

entry.title: Re: Error 403 when Create Sales Order using API_SALES_ORD...
entry.content:
Hi,

First, even if your username and password are correct, check the user roles. The user needs proper permissions to create sales orders. They might be able to get information, but not create it. Look at the user’s roles in SAP S/4HANA to see if they can create sales orders.

Another thing to check is the CSRF Token. You mentioned using X-CSRF-Token for POST requests. This token is important for security and expires after a while. If it’s old, that could cause the 403 Forbidden error. Make sure to get a new CSRF token from the initial GET request and use it right away in your POST request. If you're making this request from a web app, you might face CORS issues. Postman can get around this, but a web app often can't. Make sure your SAP system is set up to allow cross-origin requests.

Also, double-check the headers in your request. The Content-Type should be application/json, and the Accept header needs to match the response format. Sometimes, missing or wrong headers can lead to authorization errors. Lastly, check the API logs in SAP S/4HANA. They can give you more info on the 403 Forbidden error and whether it’s a permission problem or something else. Asked Business Central consultants https://www.hiredynamicsdevelopers.com/hire-microsoft-dynamics-business-central-consultants/ from my company and gathered some tips that might help you fix the issue.

entry.title: Navigating the Future of Technology: SAP Build Code, SAP Joule, LLMs, and Prompt Engineering
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced tech world, innovations like SAP Build Code, SAP Joule, Large Language Models (LLMs), and Prompt Engineering are fundamentally transforming the digital landscape. Whether you’re a developer, data scientist, or tech enthusiast, staying up-to-date on these emerging technologies is essential. In this blog, we’ll break down each of these cutting-edge concepts.

What is SAP Build Code?

SAP Build Code is a low-code/no-code platform designed to streamline application development within the SAP ecosystem.

  • Purpose: This platform empowers both developers and business users to design, build, and deploy enterprise-grade applications with minimal coding effort.
  • Key Features: It offers a drag-and-drop interface, pre-built templates, seamless integration with SAP systems like S/4HANA and BTP, and robust workflow automation.
  • Why It Matters: SAP Build Code democratizes application development, allowing faster innovation while reducing the reliance on IT departments.

What is SAP Joule?

SAP Joule is an AI-powered digital assistant designed to enhance enterprise productivity and decision-making.

  • Key Features: Joule integrates seamlessly within SAP applications, providing contextual recommendations, real-time insights, and automating routine tasks.
  • Use Cases: Whether it’s automating workflows or delivering actionable business insights, Joule is revolutionizing how businesses leverage AI for operational efficiency.

For an in-depth exploration, check out my blog: What is SAP Joule?

What Are Large Language Models (LLMs)?

Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT-4 and Bard are advanced AI models designed to understand and generate human-like text.

  • Core Technology: These models are built on Transformer architecture and use vast datasets, enabling them to process language and generate text that mimics human communication.
  • Applications: LLMs are used in chatbots, automated content creation, sentiment analysis, machine translation, and much more.
  • Significance: LLMs are transforming natural language understanding (NLU), reshaping the way we interact with machines and automating complex tasks.

What is Prompt Engineering?

Prompt Engineering is the process of crafting specific inputs (prompts) to guide AI models like LLMs toward generating the desired output.

  • Key Principles:
  • Be Specific: Clear, well-defined prompts lead to more accurate results.
  • Use Context: Providing context helps the model understand the intent behind the query.
  • Iterative Refinement: Experimenting with different prompts optimizes the response quality.
  • Why It Matters: Mastering prompt engineering is essential for harnessing the full potential of AI tools, from solving problems to fostering creativity.

entry.title: Re: Error en POST VendorPayments: "value": "Unbalanced ca...
entry.content: Le pasé la pretunta a ChatGPT y dijo esto: El error "Unbalanced cash flow assignment" que estás experimentando al intentar enviar un pago mediante el Service Layer de SAP Business One indica que hay un desajuste en la asignación del flujo de caja en tu JSON. A continuación, se señalan posibles causas y recomendaciones para resolver este problema: 1. **Inconsistencias en los montos**: - Asegúrate de que los valores de `CashSum`, `CashSumFC` y los montos especificados en las secciones de `PaymentAccounts` y `CashFlowAssignments` sean coherentes y sumen correctamente. - Verifica que los montos en moneda local (LC) y moneda extranjera (FC) estén correctamente calculados según el tipo de cambio aplicable. 2. **Asignación de flujo de caja**: - Revisa que la asignación en `CashFlowAssignments` esté equilibrada. Es decir, la suma de los créditos y débitos debe coincidir con los montos totales del documento. - Asegúrate de que cada línea en `CashFlowAssignments` esté correctamente asociada con las líneas correspondientes en `PaymentAccounts`. 3. **Configuración del flujo de caja en SAP Business One**: - Verifica la configuración del flujo de caja en SAP Business One. Si la asignación de flujo de caja está configurada como "Obligatoria", cualquier discrepancia puede generar este error. Considera cambiar esta opción a "Opcional" en la pestaña de Flujo de Caja dentro de las Configuraciones Generales, bajo el módulo de Administración. citeturn0search7 4. **Actualizaciones y notas de SAP**: - Consulta las notas de SAP relacionadas con este error. Por ejemplo, la nota 1538223 aborda problemas similares en versiones anteriores a la PL18. Aunque esta nota ha sido descontinuada, puede ofrecer información útil. citeturn0search2 5. **Revisión de cuentas contables**: - Asegúrate de que las cuentas contables utilizadas en el JSON estén correctamente configuradas y sean coherentes con las transacciones que estás intentando procesar. Si después de estas revisiones el problema persiste, te recomiendo contactar al soporte de SAP o consultar con un experto en SAP Business One para una asistencia más detallada.

Hi @RafaelHernandez 

Currently, within the LMS application, it is not possible to have attachments added to Learning History in mass / bulk. It can only be done manually, one by one.

There have been some enhancements requests on it, and the customers are welcomed to vote for them and/or create additional requests:

  • Bulk import of documents to multiple learning histories
  • mass upload attachment

For more details on enhancement requests, please check the KBA 2090228 - How to submit enhancement ideas for SAP SuccessFactors Products.

Best regards,

entry.title: SAP SPM Incentive Management - Advanced Workflow White Paper - Custom Actions in GCP
entry.content:
Introduction

This document provides guidance on creating and executing Advanced Workflow Custom Actions on the new GCP platform

This document assumes that you have prior knowledge and experience with Advanced Workflow application. You will also need Administration permissions in Advanced Workflow application on your GCP Tenant. 

Custom actions help users move from a parent form to a child form to find specific information, such as a customer id, using details like the customer’s first name, last name, or email ID. This keeps the parent form simple and organized. For example, users can click a button on the parent form to navigate to a child form, enter details, search for the customer ID, and bring the information back to the parent form. 

Steps to Implement: 

  1. Prepare the workflow - Set up a workflow with necessary fields. 
  2. Create the custom action - Define an action and configure its behaviour. 
  3. Attach the custom action - Link the custom action to the parent form. 
  4. Submit the child form - Implement script logic for data transfer. 

Step 1: Prepare the workflow

Log in to the workflow system and create a sample workflow named Custom Action. In the Case Create action, add custom fields like Customer ID to the form. The final form is displayed in the screenshot below. 

Note: This document does not cover the steps for creating a workflow or custom fields.  

Step 2: Create the Custom Action

In this step, we will create a custom action. Navigate to Form Designer, add fields like First Name, Last Name, and Customer ID, then add a button, set its behavior, and save the form

From Workflow, go to Setup > FIELDS & FORM > Custom Actions

Click on the "Add New" button and enter the action name, such as "Customer Details". 

Click “Save and go to Form Designer” and design the child form with fields for First Name, Last Name, and Customer ID.

Add a button to the form, select "Submit and Return to Root Form" and click “Save”. 

The following button behaviors are used exclusively with custom actions: 

Submit Form: Saves data and returns user to the previous form. The previous form relates to the action from which the custom action was called. 

Submit and Back to Root Form: Saves data and returns the user to the first action from which initial custom action was called. 

Submit all Forms: Submit all data that user enters and completes the execution of the root action. The root action is the one from which the custom action was called. 

We have successfully created the custom action and will now attach it to the parent form.

Step 3: Attach the Custom Action

In this step, we will navigate to the custom action workflow to attach the custom action to the button. 

Go to Workflow > Form Designer, add a button, select the button behavior as Execute Custom Action, and attach the custom action created in the previous steps. 

The following screenshot shows the form with the attached custom action. 

Step 4: Submit the Child Form

In this step, we will create a script and add it to the custom action's On Submit event. The script will take the Customer ID from the child form and update it in the parent form. For testing, we will enter a value in the child form, and after submission, the Customer ID will be set in the parent form. 

From Workflow, go to Setup > DEVELOPMENT > Scripts and create a new script of Form Event Type with name “ca - get customer id” (with system Id = ca_get_customer_id)

Next, copy and paste the following code to your script: 

 

 

def cid = form.getValue('ca_child_customer_id');
parentForm.setValue('ca_customer_id',cid);

 

 

Add the script to the custom action's On Submit event. 

From Workflow, go to Setup > FIELDS & FORMS > Custom Actions, click Edit Button, attach the script to the submit event and then click the Update button. 

Go to the custom action workflow and click the Customer Details button. This will navigate to another form. Enter the Customer ID and click the Submit button. Upon submission, the form will return to the parent form, and the Customer ID from the child form will be displayed in the parent form. 

 

Conclusion

Custom actions enhance workflow flexibility by allowing seamless navigation between parent and child forms. By implementing custom actions, users can efficiently retrieve and update specific information, such as Customer ID, while keeping the parent form clean and organized. This structured approach improves usability, streamlines data entry, and enhances the overall user experience.

References:

Advanced Workflow Custom Actions online documentation: 

Workflow Custom Actions Help

entry.title: Re: How to Find all my z programs or customised tcode, pr...
entry.content:
three  options:
1. Usually if we know the output type, then we got to TNAPR table and find which program is triggering the output and SMARTFORM.

2. Sometime if you know the closest name of smartform , then check table STXFADM - this will have all the smartforms read the relevant .

3. Using tcode - code_scanner  search in Z* package - CALL FUNCTION - SSF_FUNCTION**

entry.title: Re: How to Find all my z programs or customised tcode, pr...

Try
1. Usually if we know the output type, then we got to TNAPR table and find which program is triggering the output and SMARTFORM.
2. Sometime if you know the closest name or Desc of smartform , then check table STXFADM - this is master table. 
3. Using tcode - code_scanner  search in Z* package - search CALL FUNCTION - SSF_FUNCTION**

entry.title: TPM15M for long-term contracts
entry.content:
Hi, experts,

I am facing the following issue:

The client has a long-term Money Market contract that does not capitalize interest and was initially classified with a general long-term valuation class. This contract does not have a payment schedule, which requires all interest to be accounted for directly in long-term liability accounts.

However, when there is a forecasted payment of an extraordinary amortization within a one-year period, it is necessary to transfer both the amortization amounts and the interest from long-term to short-term accounts. When executing TPM15M, only the forecasted amortization amounts are correctly transferred from long-term to short-term. As for the interest, although the nominal interest movements undergo a change in the general valuation class, there is no clear accounting movement between liability accounts.

I have searched SAP Notes and other references, and everything indicates that the transfer between accounting accounts occurs only for movements that impact the position. This would explain the absence of accounting movement for interest, as there is no effective change in the contract position.

Obs.: We utilize the 'Date-based assignment' procedure for interest.

Could you confirm if there is a way to perform this transfer? If so, could you provide guidance on how to proceed?

I appreciate your help in advance!

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Using BADI IDOC_DATA_INSERT for populating Z fields in VBAK table from IDOC document
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm glad you find this blog interesting or useful.

This blog is about our use case where we needed to have some fields from IDOC filled into appended additional fields in VBAK and VBAP tables. This blog only covers update of VBAK table. 

However, if you create and activate BADI IDOC_DATA_INSERT or BADI IDOC_DATA_MAPPER they will be called when processing IDOC but before any FM like in your case IDOC_INPUT_ORDERS. So, you could change or map IDOC data before processing it. It should be similar like in this blog since you will be dealing with EDIDD tables.

If this approached do not suffice your need you should explore explicit or implicit enhancement of FM in question, if possible.

I hope I helped,
Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: will Deprecated scope items still be usable?
entry.content:
Hi @AnitaS ,

A scope item is a business process orchestrated by SAP with one or more apps to accomplish it in a standard way (best practice). For whatever the reason, if a scope item is deprecated, as long as these apps are there, you can still orchestrate your process the same way as the original scope item.

I think the important thing is the apps, if their changes will impact your execution.  That's the area you need to pay attention to. 

Regards,

George

entry.title: SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Silicon Valley highlighted how AI makes app development easier
entry.content:
Thanks to all the local customers who joined us for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Silicon Valley on February 5, 2025. By the end of the day, everyone saw the latest and greatest AI innovations that guide business users through their SAP tasks more quickly and make developers more productive by automating and accelerating key parts of their work.

For many, the highlight was the hands-on session where they had the opportunity to build an SAP Fiori app, starting with a sketch of what they want the app to look like. They learned about generative AI, the Flexible Programming Model in SAP Fiori elements, and how to fine-tune apps using SAP Fiori tools.

My favorite part of the event were the conversations with customers during the breaks where I had a chance to learn more about their specific industries and the unique challenges they face. This reinforces the need for some level of customization in the starting page, induvial apps, and business processes.

Schedule for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Silicon Valley

The day started with an overview of SAP Fiori and what this looks like in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Similar to how you would begin your day with SAP, we started with My Home and all the different ways you have to launch your SAP Fiori apps and make the page your own.

Graphic showing personalization options in My Home by creating the cards you want to see

We then showed examples of the SAP Fiori user experience across SAP S/4HANA, including many of the innovations that we have recently added to our public cloud version. We also presented several ways on how to create cards to show key information in My Home.

SAP Fiori includes more AI capabilities for business users

In the morning, we showed all the released AI functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, much of which we introduced in 2502.

  • Easy filter – natural language searching in SAP Fiori elements list reports
  • Smart summarization – AI-created summary of SAP Fiori elements object pages
  • Card creation in My Home– several ways to create a card that you can add to My Home
  • Joule-assisted insight card creation in My Home– ask Joule for the information you need and create a card that you can add to My Home

We ended the day with some previews of planned AI functionality that will first be released into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

  • Recap – a summary of what you missed when you were away from your SAP system
  • Assisted error resolution – providing more context on system errors with recommendations to resolve them
  • Microsoft CoPilot integration

Some of these are currently in beta testing with selected public cloud customers.

Clean core is how you can extend your SAP S/4HANA system.

One important area that many customers are interested in hearing about is how to extend their SAP S/4HANA systems. This could be creating new apps or moving existing customizations from their on-prem environments into their private cloud implementation. There are many options, some on-stack in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and some on the side in BTP.

Graphic of the options to extend your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system in a clean core way

RISE extensibility guide

GROW extensibility guide

The partners shared their vison for the future and how to get there

Event gold sponsors cbs, Lemongrass, and Qubittron presented about various use cases for AI and how they have helped customers overcome initial hesitation. They also shared their wisdom in a panel discussion that covered different dimensions of UX transformation along with SAP Fiori adoption, change management, and how to gain alignment among different stakeholders in an organization.

Thanks to everyone who made SAP Fiori Innovation Day a success

SAP Presenters (in order of appearance): Sebastian Steinhauer, Katja Zoch, Chad Leonard, Vikas Lodha, Dan Watters, Carolina Robertson, Sushant Priyadarshi, Conrad Bernal, Ashley Tung, Marcel Kassner

Gold partner sponsors: Qubittron, cbs Corporate Business Solutions, lemongrass

Silver partner sponsor: qBotica

Behind the scenes team: all the developers, architects, product managers and other colleagues who built the functionality and set up the demos and hands-on systems.

Without all the great customer interaction, questions, and conversations during meals and breaks, we would not have learned what’s most important to you as you move forward in each of your respective organizations in your UX transformation projects.

 

Next steps

  • Register for the monthly SAP Fiori development roundtable call to meet other developers and architects who build SAP Fiori apps.
  • Subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter, in which we share updates on SAP Fiori elements, SAP Fiori tools, SAPUI5, and SAP Build Code every other month.  
  • Read about last year’s SAP Fiori Innovation Days events in Heidelberg, Chicago, and Dublin.
  • Join us at an SAP Fiori Development Innovation Day in 2025 – we are finalizing the locations and dates.

entry.title: Re: Assignment Profile Behaviour with Items, Library, Curricula
entry.content:
Hi @DeeCrews 

I think it's easier if we split the entities that get assigned through Assignment Profiles in two categories, just for illustration purposes:

  • Learning Plan (items, programs, curricula);
  • Libraries.

When assigning Libraries to the users through APs, that affects the entities the users can search by themselves at the user side (through the Enhanced Search and/or the legacy library).

And the "Learning Plan" entities get assigned to the users' Learning Plans, which is something that can be double-checked by navigating to Learning Administration > People > search and open a user's record > Assigned Items tab or Curricula tab or Program tab (depending on what's being assigned).

  • These entities will or will not be seen by the users on the user side depending on other settings. For example: curricula that are not Required nor have an Effective date are considered as Complete by the system (https://me.sap.com/notes/2252538); hence won't be visible to the user at the user side. But it doesn't mean it hasn't been assigned by the AP.

Maybe to start you can configure the AP, propagate its changes, keep the Assignment Profile Execute Updates job enabled and running (so the users get included / removed from the AP when they meet or fall out of the rules), and then check the records of users that are part of the AP, looking at their Assigned Items, Curricula, Programs, Library Access tabs to see if the AP's entities were assigned. If shown there, it means the AP worked and then you can look into the specifics of what's not shown at the user side (what setting is interfering, etc.).

--See Also--

  • 2747663 - An Overview of Assignment Profiles in SuccessFactors Learning 
  • 2282363 - Assignment profile (AP) not assigning program to the users who are a part of the AP 
  • 2392503 - Assignment Profile Execute Changes vs Update Users and Assignments 
  • 2073591 - Assignment Profile is no longer re-assigning entities 
  • 2286448 - How to tell if an assignment profile is propagated in Learning? 
  • Assigning Libraries to Learners 

Best regards,
